parts.content:
Write 1 dissimilar SBA question(s) on: 'Anaesthetic depth is related more closely to partial pressure than volume percent.'.

Each question should test application of knowledge, not simple recall — a candidate should need to reason from principles rather than just retrieve a fact. Use the retrieve tool to find relevant reference material before writing. Return the result as a QuestionSet.

parts.args: {"query": 'Anaesthetic depth is related more closely to partial pressure than volume percent.'}

{"topic":"Primary Frca Sample Sbas","exam":"primary","curriculum_node_code":null,"model":"openai:gpt-5.5","generated_at":"2026-05-02T01:16:45.156172","questions":[{"stem":"A sevoflurane vaporiser calibrated at sea level is used in a hospital at 3000 metres altitude without adjustment. Anaesthesia using a dial setting of 1.7–2% sevoflurane in air appears uneventful.","lead":"Which statement best explains why the vaporiser may be used at similar dial settings at high altitude and sea level?","options":[{"text":"Depth of anaesthesia is titrated against clinical response not anaesthetic concentration","is_correct":false,"explanation":"This is true clinically, but it does not explain the physical behaviour of the vaporiser with altitude."},{"text":"Monitors of end tidal vapour concentration compensate for altitude","is_correct":false,"explanation":"End-tidal agent monitors may display appropriately, but monitoring does not explain vaporiser output."},{"text":"Plenum vaporisers deliver the same partial pressure at any particular dial setting irrespective of ambient pressure","is_correct":true,"explanation":"Variable-bypass plenum vaporisers effectively deliver a fixed vapour partial pressure for a given dial setting, so the clinically relevant partial pressure is preserved at altitude."},{"text":"Sevoflurane concentration at the vaporiser output increases as ambient pressure decreases","is_correct":false,"explanation":"This may occur when a fixed partial pressure is delivered at lower barometric pressure, but it is a consequence rather than the best explanation."},{"text":"The temperature drop at higher altitudes has only minimal effect on vaporiser function","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Temperature compensation is important, but this does not best explain altitude independence of dial settings."}],"explanation":"Anaesthetic depth is related more closely to partial pressure than volume percent. Modern plenum vaporisers are calibrated to deliver a predictable vapour partial pressure at a given setting, making similar dial settings usable at altitude, although displayed volume percent may change.","curriculum_node_codes":["1_GA_F2_16","1_GA_B_18","1_GA_G2_56","1_GA_F3_15","1_GA_F1_8"],"curriculum_node_scores":[0.6552015944628167,0.6117488863081917,0.6111315981931682,0.6086284338761914,0.5959277484372407]}]}
